grant_title: Understanding and Addressing Health Disparities in Rural and Urban Communities: Bridging the Geographic Divide
geographic_restrictions: No explicit geographic restrictions mentioned; proposals should address health disparities in both rural and urban communities in the United States.
project_area: Health disparities reduction, rural and urban healthcare access, telehealth, community health, maternal health, chronic disease management, mental health, healthcare workforce development
demographic_requirements: Proposals should prioritize underserved populations, including rural residents, urban disadvantaged communities, low-income individuals, racial and ethnic minorities, and other marginalized groups facing healthcare access barriers.
eligibility_summary: Eligible applicants include nonprofit organizations, for-profit entities, government agencies, academic institutions, community health centers, healthcare providers, and public health agencies. Projects must focus on reducing health disparities in rural and/or urban communities through innovative healthcare delivery models, workforce development, infrastructure improvements, or policy solutions.
